title: Let Our Gladness Have No End
abc_source:
X: 62
T: Let Our Gladness Have No End
%%combinevoices 1
C: Words: From the Kancional, by Tobias Zavorka, 1602.  Translator unknown. 
C: Music: 'Narodil se Kristus Pán' or 'Salvator Natus' 15th Century Bohemian. 
C: Setting: "Carols Old And Carols New", 1916.
C: copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2007 Revision.
S: Music source: Carols Old And Carols New, Boston: Parish Choir, Charles L. Hutchins, 1916 Hymn 577.
S: Translation is public domain per LSB 2006 Hymn 381

w: 2.~See, the love- liest bloom- ing rose, Hal- le- lu- jah! 
w: 3.~In- to flesh is made the Word. Hal- le- lu- jah!

% 5
[V: S1V1]  d d c c | B B A2 | B c A2 | G3 z | B A B c |
w: For to earth did Christ de- scend. Hal- le- lu- jah! On this day God 
w: From the branch of Je- sse grows. Hal- le- lu- jah! * * * *
w: He, our re- fuge and our Lord. Hal- le- lu- jah!  * * * *

metadata.composer: 'Narodil se Kristus Pán' or 'Salvator Natus' 15th Century Bohemian "Carols Old And Carols New", 1916
metadata.lyricist: From the Kancional, by Tobias Zavorka, 1602.  Translator unknown

c_lines: Words: From the Kancional, by Tobias Zavorka, 1602.  Translator unknown.
Music: 'Narodil se Kristus Pán' or 'Salvator Natus' 15th Century Bohemian.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2007 Revision.

1.raw_text: Let our gladness have no end, Hallelujah! For to earth did Christ descend. Hallelujah! On this day God gave us Christ, His Son, to save us; Christ, His Son, to save us.

2.raw_text: See, the loveliest blooming rose, Hallelujah! From the branch of Jesse grows. Hallelujah! gave us Christ, His Son, to save us; Christ, His Son, to save us.

3.raw_text: Into flesh is made the Word. Hallelujah! He, our refuge and our Lord. Hallelujah! gave us Christ, His Son, to save us; Christ, His Son, to save us.
